title: walshia kendallii
intro: Walshia kendallii, also known as the Red Sandalwood, is a critically endangered plant species native to the Western Ghats region of India. This plant is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which are derived from its heartwood. The health benefits of Walshia kendallii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared as an essential oil, powder, or decoction for various herbal preparations, including topical creams, ointments, and medicinal teas, although its availability and sustainability are major concerns due to over-harvesting.
